note: Mathlib/Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Trigonometric/Chebyshev/Orthogonality.lean:131 — proves Chebyshev T polynomials are orthogonal w.r.t. measureT (a specific weighted measure); this is one family, not the general theory of orthogonal polynomials (no such general concept/def exists in Mathlib).
verify_note: Decl confirmed at cited line (theorem integral_eval_T_real_mul_eval_T_real_measureT_of_ne {n m : ℕ} (h : n ≠ m) : ∫ x, (T ℝ n).eval x * (T ℝ m).eval x ∂measureT = 0); Wikidata Q619458 = 'orthogonal polynomials', 'set of polynomials where any two are orthogonal to each other' — Chebyshev T is honestly one instance, no general Mathlib def exists, special_case is the correct (non-inflated) label.

note: Mathlib/Computability/Halting.lean:65 — theorem halting_problem (n) : ¬ComputablePred fun c => (eval c n).Dom, docstring '**Halting problem** is not computable'; this IS the undecidability-of-the-halting-problem theorem.
verify_note: Decl confirmed (namespace ComputablePred, line 65, docstring literally names it 'Halting problem'); Wikidata Q622849 description 'problem of determining whether a given program will finish running or continue forever' matches; Mathlib has no separate reified 'halting problem' object, so the undecidability theorem is the canonical formalization — exact is honest.

note: Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Dimension/StrongRankCondition.lean:678 — 'An extension of rings R ⊆ S is quadratic if S is a free R-algebra of rank 2'; general ring/field extension class (a quadratic field is the special case IsQuadraticExtension ℚ K with K a field), no ℚ-specific 'quadratic field' def exists.
verify_note: Decl name is not the full dotted name — the class lives at line 679 inside 'namespace Algebra ... end Algebra' (grep confirms no export alias), so it is Algebra.IsQuadraticExtension, not bare IsQuadraticExtension; the cited row cannot be resolved as written and fails the decl-existence check regardless of the otherwise-plausible generalization semantics.

note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/Extremal/RuzsaSzemeredi.lean:50 — noncomputable def ruzsaSzemerediNumber, 'the maximum number of edges a locally linear graph on that type can have'; file also proves the asymptotic lower bound (ruzsaSzemerediNumberNat_asymptotic_lower_bound), i.e. the actual RS problem.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at cited line 50; Wikidata Q62760546 description confirms 'maximum number of edges in a graph where each edge belongs to exactly one triangle' — locally linear is exactly 'each edge in exactly one triangle', so ruzsaSzemerediNumber is precisely the quantity the problem asks about; exact is warranted.
